content_body:
Expect big experiences at an affordable cost in our luxury Blue Ridge cabin rentals

Planning a cabin trip in the North GA Mountains, but not wanting to break the bank ~ Check out our budget friendly Blue Ridge cabins in Georgia and you will discover so many fun things to do in the area that will keep you coming back to discover more! We offer an assorted array of discounts and specials that will help you plan.

Cabin trips in Georgia are not only affordable at Cabin Rentals of Georgia, but the memories you make here will be priceless.

1.) Choose an affordable cabin: We have our rustic retreat on 30 private acres named Cherokee Sunrise on Noontootla Creek, a renovated 100-year old farmhouse with a fishing pond and fire pit. 

 

Aska Escape Lodge is a very popular affordable luxury cabin in the heart of the Aska Adventure Area close to all the fun outdoor activities along the Toccoa River and in the Blue Ridge Mountains. 

 

2.) Stay with a group: Band together with a group of friends to make a budget friendly Blue Ridge retreat! Split the cost with 3 or 4 couples and stay at High Hopes or Blue Ridge Lake Sanctuary to have a luxurious experience at half the cost. Split the cost with five couples - fabulous Fallen Timber Lodge, or royal Riverview Lodge. The more, the merrier - we're here to make all of it happen!

 

3.) Cut costs with a creative night in: You didn't come to the mountains for the nightlife, so bring the nightlife to you. All of our cabins have gaming amenities like arcade games, pool tables, or table-top games. There's always the opportunity to have a fun night in by putting on your favorite Spotify playlist, building a blanket fort, or taking advantage of the cabin amenities like hot tubs, fire pits, game rooms, or even create hiking trails in the areas around the cabin. As most cabins are pet friendly, make more memories with the entire family by planning a fun night in. The space is more than just sleeping room space, it is an experience to be enjoyed, a moment to be remembered!

 

4) Get Out and Get Wild - Finding cheap and free things to do:

  • Toccoa River Swinging Bridge is arguably among the most iconic attractions in Blue Ridge, a short drive into the Appalachians takes you to the longest bridge east of the Mississippi River.

  • Up-cycle your leftovers from the Blue Ridge Mountains Food Tours and kill two birds with one stone, something to do, and dinner for later! They have three different tours - The Fabulous Foodie Tour, Raise Your Glass Tour, and the Sugar High Tour.

  • If you're staying at our cabins along the Toccoa River, put in a tube and float for 1.5 hours to Horseshoe Bend Park and beyond into McCaysville.

  • If you're staying at our luxury cabins along the Toccoa River, enjoy fly-fishing right from the property.

  • Build a nice trail riding course on the way back to your cabin by checking out the Long Branch trails, a 2.3 mile hiking loop right here in Blue Ridge.
     

5.) Vacation Rental Specials and Money-saving tips: Check out our special offers!  Be sure to check out our availability calendar to see pricing matrixes throughout the year honoring off-seasons and daily pricing. Save a significant amount of money from other travel agencies by calling and booking directly through one of our reservation specialists. We look forward to helping you plan a memorable trip to Blue Ridge!
title: 5 Tips for a Budget-Friendly Vacation in our Blue Ridge Cabin Rentals
